programme.title: Movie: The Parent Trap
programme.desc: In this remake of the classic Disney film, identical twins mischievously conspire to reunite their unhappily divorced parents.

programme.title: E! News
programme.desc: Lady Antebellum is in the studio. Plus, it's been 25 years since 'Friends' premiered, we've got new interviews with the cast and secrets from the set. Then, our new interview with 'The Voice' coaches.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Confessions of a Beauty Queen
programme.desc: With Savannah and Chase leaving the nest in the next few years, Todd and Julie decide it's time to downsize.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Chase Turns 18
programme.desc: After 18 years of counting down the days, Chase is finally 18! When he tells Todd that he will no longer follow the house rules such as a curfew, Todd decides it’s time to give his son a dose of reality and show him what it’s really like to be an adult.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Anger Management
programme.desc: Todd blames his family for his stress when his doctor tells him that he has high blood pressure. Savannah gives Todd more bad news. Nothing works until Todd finds his own way to relieve his stress, leaving the kids running for cover.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: California Chrisleys
programme.desc: The Chrisleys venture to LA for their regular Beverly Hills haircuts, and for what Todd hopes will be a relaxing family vacation. His plans and sanity are threatened by new levels of Chrisley family craziness when Savannah does something drastic.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: Workin' for a Livin'
programme.desc: After Todd unknowingly foots the bill for an extravagant lunch, he wants Chase and Savannah to learn that money doesn't grow on trees. Forced to get summer jobs, the kids struggle to face the reality of a hard day's work for the first time.

programme.title: Chrisley Knows Best: The Great Outdoors
programme.desc: Todd and Chase are forced to experience the great outdoors when Grayson insists on going camping. While the guys 'rough it' in a luxury RV and terrify Grayson with stories of a haunted lake, Savannah and Julie must go back to driving school.

programme.title: E! News
programme.desc: The biggest and most talked about stories of the day in pop culture.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Frenemies
programme.desc: Miranda is shocked to learn that her date has died; Carrie teaches a seminar.

programme.title: Sex and the City: What Goes Around Comes Around
programme.desc: Carrie apologizes to Natasha, and Charlotte encounters a gardener at the McDougal estate.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Cock a Doodle Do!
programme.desc: When Big invites Carrie to lunch, she ignores Miranda's advice and accepts his invitation.

programme.title: Sex and the City: The Agony and the Ex-Tacy
programme.desc: Carrie is left alone at a restaurant on her birthday and ponders the idea of soulmates.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Defining Moments
programme.desc: Carrie meets a jazzman to help her get over Big; Samantha crosses some boundaries.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Time and Punishment
programme.desc: Carrie frets about Aidan's ability to forgive her affair; Miranda discovers leisure.

programme.title: Sex and the City: My Motherboard, Myself
programme.desc: Carrie and Aidan exchange keys; Miranda strains to maintain her strong woman image.

programme.title: Sex and the City: Sex and the Country
programme.desc: Samantha joins Carrie at Aidan's cottage in the country where she meets a cute farmer.

programme.title: Flip It Like Disick: Family Matters
programme.desc: With Jed Smith officially purchased and the new layout for the property agreed upon, Scott is eager to get started with demo right away, but Willa and Miki are at odds.

programme.title: Flip It Like Disick: Miki vs. Malibu
programme.desc: After visiting a mega-flip property being built by Scott's real estate mentor, Ilan, Scott is inspired to redo the pool at his personal residence.

programme.title: Flip It Like Disick: Jungle Rules
programme.desc: With Miki feeling disrespected and a potential HOA shutdown looming, the team now faces major personal and logistical problems. Scott helps French Montana transform one of his rooms into a jungle-themed theater.

programme.title: Flip It Like Disick: Unexpected Help
programme.desc: With the HOA shutdown still keeping Jed Smith at a standstill, Willa and Benny renovate their offices. Scott looks for ways to make up for lost time and brings in outside help without telling Benny or Miki.

programme.title: Flip It Like Disick: More Money, More Problems
programme.desc: Scott and team encounter a problem at Jed Smith that could jeopardize the entire project. Scott attempts to get away from all the drama at Jed Smith by working with Willa to redesign his daughter Penelope's bedroom.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Fire Escape
programme.desc: When devastating wildfires erupt in their neighborhood, the family is forced to flee from their homes, threatening to ruin the fairytale birthday party Rob is planning for his daughter. Kim awaits the president's word on her prison reform legislation.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Pet Peeve
programme.desc: Khloe’s mistrust of her mother’s boyfriend, Corey, resurfaces on a very awkward family trip to Palm Springs, leaving Kris hurt and confused about her daughter’s behavior. Meanwhile, the family stays with Kendall to escape the fires.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Unhappy Camper
programme.desc: Determined to fulfil one of North's lifelong dreams, Kim puts her survival skills to the test on an overnight camping trip in the wilderness. Malika heads back to Cleveland for the first time since True's birth and suspects Khloe is struggling internally.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Christmas Chaos
programme.desc: Kris reluctantly allows Kim to take over the annual Christmas Eve Party, but the holiday is in jeopardy when Kris learns Kim has invited an unwelcome guest. Khloe must come to the rescue when North’s beloved hamster unexpectedly dies and Kim can’t deal.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Birthdays and Bad News Part 1
programme.desc: Khloe invites her ex to True's first birthday party, but an uncomfortable confrontation in front of family and friends leaves her questioning her decision.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Birthdays and Bad News Part 2
programme.desc: After an uncomfortable confrontation with her ex at True's first birthday, Khloe must figure out how to coexist with her ex for her daughter's sake.

programme.title: Movie: Hitch
programme.desc: While helping his latest client, a professional 'date doctor' falls for a journalist.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Passin' the Baton
programme.desc: Guy's hitting' the road and chowin' down on some long standing traditions.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Flavortown Medley
programme.desc: A Memphis bar-turned-bistro offers low brow favourites; a food truck in Hawaii.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Roadtrippin' In Beantown
programme.desc: This trip, Guy is in Beantown for an all-Boston smorgasbord. Just outside the city in Allston, two restaurants share an open kitchen but have completely different menus.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: From Standards to Standouts
programme.desc: Guy is hitting up all kinds of joints, from classic Italian to Modern Americana. In Jackson, a California chef is bringing his brand of Mexican to the mountains.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Turkey, Taters And Dogs
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri's digging into a righteous mashup of flavors. In Orlando, Fla. Joey Fatone is riding shotgun to a potato-centric palace, plus a trip to his very own hot dog hangout.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Chicken Trifecta
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri's diggin' into some winner winner chicken dinners! In Maui, Hawaii, it's a star-studded stop at Mick Fleetwood's joint serving the rocker's mom's favourite chicken, plus a dish that musician Sammy Hagar dives right into.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Italian, Hawaiian and Colombian
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy Fieri's digging into a multicultural flavour fest. In Hawaii, a poke place is knocking it out of the park with their island specialties. In San Carlos, California, a crazy Italian spot putting tasty twists on their homemade pasta.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Lights-Out Latin
programme.desc: This trip Guy's into all sorts of amazing Latin specialties from Peru to Panama to elsewhere.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Triple D Nation: Coastal Cuisine
programme.desc: The sun is out and the surf is high as Guy Fieri hits up some ultimate DDD spots by the shore. First, he's in Honolulu to say 'Hi' to an old buddy who's reelin' in fresh poke and smokin' pork by the plateful.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Ocean Coasts and Great Lakes
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's going from coast to coast and to the Great Lakes to dig up some dynamite dishes. In Los Angeles, a Mexican joint in Grand Central Market serves outrageous octopus tostadas and puts a lights-out spin on a burger.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Spinning the Globe
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri's checking out joints putting their own spin on global flavour. In Kauai, Hawaii, a culinary dojo ampin' up local specialties like saimin and chile pepper chicken. An outrageous twist on tacos and burritos in Kansas City, Mo.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Barbecue, Batter and Beer Can Chicken
programme.desc: Maui, Hawaii has ridiculous ramen and beer can chicken. Los Angeles has a food truck with a twist on fish and chips. Plus, a barbecue spot with fried chicken.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Breakfast, Brisket and Belly
programme.desc: Guy is in Los Angeles at a late-night spot with a twist, Columbus for a joint serving creative combos like brisket on corn flakes and Phoenix at a barbeque stop with smoked prime rib and pork belly dumplings.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Old Faces, New Places
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri's visiting some familiar Triple D faces and checking out their new places including a primo pizza joint in Flagstaff, Arizona firing up pies with not-your-everyday toppings.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa: Easy as Pie
programme.desc: Ina is sharing pie-making secrets with her friend Michael in exchange for some floristry magic. There are raspberry muffins to welcome him, and Ina shows him how to make the perfect piecrust.

programme.desc: Giada arrives in Florence, Italy and heads straight to the local market, where she shops and introduces the local cuisine.

programme.title: Trisha's Southern Kitchen: Trisha's Spicy Kitchen
programme.desc: After chowing down on some delicious but fiery hot chicken from Prince's Hot Chicken Shack, Trisha and her friend Julie make some spicy specialties, including chipotle peanuts and South tex-mex rice.

programme.title: Trisha's Southern Kitchen: Seeing Double
programme.desc: Trisha celebrates her wax figure being inducted into Nashville's Madame Tussauds Wax Museum. The menu includes ingredients that are used in at least two ways, including Pork Tenderloin with Bacon Onion Jam and Apple Cider and Sautéed Apple Basmati rice.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Quick and Easy: Take Five
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is making four meals on four days, and they all have just five ingredients. For breakfast, she whips up Chilaquiles, a Mexican breakfast with Osage County style.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman: Night Out Night In
programme.desc: With the kids out on the town at a street party, Ree Drummond and Ladd enjoy a night in. They're cooking surf and turf skewers with Cajun hollandaise, a romaine wedge salad with loaded ranch dressing and rummy pineapples and cream to keep things sweet.

programme.title: The Kitchen: Fresh Start to Fall
programme.desc: The Kitchen is starting things off fresh for the fall season with new dishes like Katie Lee's chicken parmesan zucchini boats and hasselback apples.

programme.title: Trisha's Southern Kitchen: Love For Loveless Cafe
programme.desc: One of Trisha Yearwood's favorite spots in Nashville is The Loveless Cafe. She spends the day putting a spin on her favorite dishes from the restaurant.

programme.title: Trisha's Southern Kitchen: Glam Bites
programme.desc: Trisha Yearwood takes some time to reflect on the experiences she's shared with her Glam Squad, and she shows her thanks by making some small bites perfect for entertaining. Her menu includes artichoke parmesan crostini.

programme.title: Supermarket Stakeout: You Wanna 'Pizza' Me?

programme.desc: Bobby and Sophie Flay are visiting their favorite spots for lobster in New York City, but they start out in SoHo for coffee and a sweet snack at Balthazar. They head to Bobby's pick, Ed's Lobster Bar, and grab a trendy, frozen snack at Kith Treats.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Bird and Beef

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Vegan Plant
programme.desc: Michael feeds a cannibalistic plant at a garden center, and at the tuxedo store, he sells a tie that literally suits itself. Plus, at a hockey rink, Michael's assistant is speechless with a trick so electric it melts the ice.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Sorcery or Science
programme.desc: Michael sweetens a woman's day at a candy store when a chocolate bunny eats her ring, and at the stadium for the Chicago White Sox, he inflates pretzels at a snack stand. Plus, one of Michael's tricks gets flushed down the tubes.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Laundry Day
programme.desc: The guys look for partners in crime at a supermarket and throw caution to the wind as helpless romantics. Plus, tonight’s loser has a serious wardrobe malfunction.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Mime and Punishment
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Murr and Q put the nasty in namaste when they lead guided meditations. Tonight's big loser gets boxed in with a 24-hour punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Junk in the Trunk
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Q and Murr feel the wrath of disapproving focus groups after nonsensical presentations, and try to reach out and touch strangers at the mall with anything but their hands. Plus, the guys must quit their job in front of a crowd.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Damned If You Do
programme.desc: Find out which joker once included inline skating as a skill on his resume, who was a mathlete and a bowler in high school and which joker was afraid of the dark as a child.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Up Loser's Creek
programme.desc: Find out which joker ran for Congress as a write-in candidate, which joker was a top-notch salesman before starting the show and who had a job in finance.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Rubbed the Wrong Way
programme.desc: Find out why the food court was picked for the 'stalk and text' challenge, what classic comedy inspired 'over the shoulder,' and who was lucky enough to test out the flying contraption for Joe the Genie.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Baggage Shame
programme.desc: Find out how Murr's old neighbour learns a little TMI about his personal life and which of the jokers Joe would want by his side in a fight.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Weight Loss
programme.desc: Adam illustrates all the reasons low-fat diets can actually make you fatter, and why counting calories is a waste of time.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Nutrition
programme.desc: Adam shows why vitamin supplements don't make us any healthier, and that the 'balanced breakfast' we've been sold is anything but. Later, he reveals how most of the nutritional science the media reports on isn't science at all.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Spider Man
programme.desc: Find out which of the jokers is the handiest, who hates the taste of beer, and how Q's love of animals beat out his arachnophobia and made for a better punishment.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine: The Funeral
programme.desc: The Nine-Nine gets another personnel change that could put Jake and Amy's relationship at risk. Meanwhile, Terry assists Holt with an important public relations matter. Rosa and Gina try to convince Charles to rethink his affection for a fellow officer.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine: Boyle's Hunch
programme.desc: Jake thinks Boyle might have met his soulmate in Genevieve, an art gallery owner, and he tries to help set them up. Meanwhile, Rosa deals with inter-precinct theft and Holt seeks Amy's help with an image campaign.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine: The Oolong Slayer
programme.desc: When investigating a serial killer, Jake enlists Holt's help in an attempt to solve the case off the radar. At the precinct, Rosa and Amy are forced to deal with demands from an old adversary, and Terry discovers a new obsession.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine: Halloween III
programme.desc: The third installment of Captain Holt and Jake's Halloween heist culminates with a tie-breaking competition to claim the title of 'amazing detective slash genius.' This year, Jake and Holt split the squad into two teams to help them win.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine: Into the Woods
programme.desc: Jake and Charles convince Terry to take a weekend camping trip as a needed 'babymoon,' but they soon realize it might not be the idealistic weekend they had hoped. Amy enlists Gina’s help to prepare her for an important presentation.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine: The Mattress
programme.desc: Jake and Amy convince the Captain to let them work a case together involving a new street drug, but their new relationship gets in the way. Meanwhile, at the precinct, Charles panics when he realizes he's damaged one of Holt's prized possessions.

programme.title: Tacoma FD: Full Moon Fever
programme.desc: FD braces for a night of insane full moon rescue calls. Plus, Eddie and Grannie go on active appendage duty and Ike gets bitten into an altered state.

programme.title: Tacoma FD: Old Flame
programme.desc: All New Eddie runs into his cradle-robbing first love and must decide whether to rekindle his romance with the sexy senior citizen. Then, Granny and Lucy compete to win the affections of an androgynous dispatcher and Terry discovers moisturizer.

programme.title: Laff Mobb's Laff Tracks: Soul Mom
programme.desc: Joyelle is done with dating actors, Daniel Weingarten accepts a challenge from his parents, and Yamaneika explains just how hard it is to run these streets. Plus, Rachel Feinstein's mom has soul in the 'joke of the night.'

programme.title: Laff Mobb's Laff Tracks: Worst Laid Plans
programme.desc: Rachel Feinstein, Tony Baker and Nate Jackson perform.

programme.title: Laff Mobb's Laff Tracks: The Inappropriate Barber
programme.desc: Mike Troy, Cee Jay Craxx and Paul Elia perform.

programme.title: Laff Mobb's Laff Tracks: Juicy
programme.desc: Ron G, Steven Briggs, Correy Bell and Neko White perform.

programme.title: Laff Mobb's Laff Tracks: The Laffs Are Back
programme.desc: Clayton English recalls his nightmare day job and Kira Soltanovich has a screaming match with her toddler son. Plus, Kerry Coddett gets catcalled by a toothless panhandler and Daniel Tirado daydreams about ditching his phone.

programme.title: Laff Mobb's Laff Tracks: Fingering the Man
programme.desc: Molly Austin takes a bachelorette party to the next level, and Jonathan Martin's parents give new meaning to the word 'cheap'. Plus, if you thought your family was embarrassing, Rachel Feinstein's might have them beat.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Smartest Inventions 3
programme.desc: Inventions include a blanket that absorbs flatulence, a wig made especially for cats, and a treadmill on wheels that finally makes it possible to take your indoor jogging routine outside.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Smartest Inventions 4
programme.desc: Inventions include a bra that doubles as a gas mask, a bib used for eating while driving and slippers with headlights.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Smartest Inventions 5
programme.desc: Questionably brilliant innovations in portable toilets, beer-finding technology, weapon storage and wigs for newborns.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: Smartest Inventions 6
programme.desc: Stars offer hilarious commentaries while viewing footage of the world's dumbest people.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: Japanese Waterless Folding Toilet
programme.desc: Find out which celebrity Michael named one of his wigs after and which trick required Michael's hidden hip-hop talents.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: An Elevated Ferret Alert
programme.desc: Find out which trick made it hard for Michael to walk the next day, what trick was added at the last minute after a prop broke and what animal Michael fosters during shoots.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: A Faux Pas to Wear
programme.desc: Why Michael says "shenanigans" during some tricks, his favorite trick of all time and why the crew had to shut down production for an hour.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: The Framing Canon
programme.desc: Find out which magic team member knows how to ride a unicycle, what trick almost used an alpaca and which trick nearly resulted in Michael slicing open his neck.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Drugs
programme.desc: Adam weeds through the myths of marijuana, exposes the blunt truth about the War on Drugs, and explains how prescription pills are the true gateway drug.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Prison
programme.desc: Adam and Eve shed light on jail rehabilitation, solitary confinement and the fact that corporations make money off of prison inmates.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins the Wild West
programme.desc: Adam illustrates how, despite its macho man reputation, prostitutes actually helped settle the West. He then goes on to explain why the 'All-American Cowboy' is a myth, and that the real hero of the west wasn't a person at all - it was air conditioning.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Justice
programme.desc: After getting Emily out of prison, Adam and his sister Rhea take on the justice system, shining a spotlight on the infamous McDonalds hot coffee lawsuit, and the substantial problems with the jury duty system.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Having a Baby
programme.desc: Adam dispels the myth about getting pregnant after 35, delves into breastfeeding vs. formula, and explores postpartum depression for women, and men.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Art
programme.desc: Adam poses the question of what makes great art and shows why pieces visible to the masses are revered, regardless of artistic merit. He exposes the masters as copycats, and reveals that today's art market is a moneymaking scheme.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: You're Cut Off
programme.desc: The guys pose as employees at a copy centre, conduct bizarre exit interviews for moviegoers and then lead some very peculiar protests. Later, it's unhappy hour for tonight's big loser, as he cuts off paying customers at a party.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Toasted
programme.desc: Joe, Murr, Sal and Q get in a pickle serving up humiliating tasks as deli waiters, and later, they step behind the counter asking customers to settle a series of silly debates. Plus, tonight's big loser gets burned during an embarrassing toast.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Pseudo-Sumo
programme.desc: The Jokers that are big pro wrestling fans, and how long it took Joe to recover from his Sumo punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Uncool and the Gang
programme.desc: What was Sal's fear when Joe poured water in his mouth blindfolded? What punishmnet had producers so scared, they doubled security.

programme.title: Modern Family: Clash of Swords

programme.desc: Phil stumbles into an exciting new career after dropping by Luke's college; while Claire deals with her dad, Jay, and the merger of Pritchett's Closets with a tech-savvy, hipper company.

programme.title: Modern Family: Torn Between Two Lovers
programme.desc: Haley can't seem to choose between her past and present relationships and is forced to decide. Meanwhile, Manny returns to college and dorm life while his girlfriend, Sherry, continues to stay at Jay and Gloria's.

programme.title: Modern Family: Bixby's Back
programme.desc: To avoid becoming a boring couple, Phil and Claire revisit a roleplaying rendezvous from the previous Valentine's Day. Meanwhile, Jay plans an elaborate surprise for Gloria, and an admirer comes between Cameron and Mitchell on Valentine's Day.

programme.title: Modern Family: Did the Chicken Cross the Road?
programme.desc: When Cam forgets the words to his favourite country song, he blames Mitch and decides he needs to reconnect with his roots and get a chicken. Meanwhile, Alex is up for a prestigious government job that requires a family interview.

programme.title: Modern Family: Go Bullfrogs!
programme.desc: Phil takes Haley on a college tour of his old alma mater, and Claire forces Mitchell and Cameron to take her for a fun night out with the boys. Meanwhile, Gloria and Jay deal with a situation at home that may require having 'the talk' with Manny.

programme.title: Modern Family: Treehouse
programme.desc: Gloria is jealous of another couple's activity level and demands that Jay step it up for a night of salsa dancing. Cameron takes a bet too far when Mitchell and Crispin challenge him to get a woman's number at the bar.

programme.title: Bones: The Princess and the Pear
programme.desc: When a group of students find the decomposed body of a woman wearing a medieval princess dress, the team is on the scene to investigate. With Booth incapacitated due to a back injury, Agent Payton Perotta steps in to help with the investigation.

programme.title: Bones: The Bones That Foam
programme.desc: Bungee-jumping newlyweds plunge off a bridge only to come face-to-face with a corpse lying at the bottom of a gorge. When the victim's bones begin to foam, the team has only 24 hours to investigate before the bones disintegrate all together.

programme.title: Bones: The Salt in the Wounds
programme.desc: Booth and Brennan investigate a petrified human body found buried in a mound of salt in a city de-icing truck. They discover that the victim was a pregnant 16-year-old high school volleyball player who was allegedly part of a 'pregnancy pact.'

programme.title: Movie: Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: After finally catching a criminal known to use black magic, Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson close another successful case. However, when his killing spree continues, the legendary sleuths take up the hunt again.

programme.title: Movie: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows
programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson team up to take down their nemesis, Professor Moriarty.

programme.title: Arrow: Sara

programme.title: Arrow: Corto Maltese
programme.desc: Oliver decides it's time for Thea to come back to Starling City, so he packs for Corto Maltese where Felicity has traced Thea's whereabouts.

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans: No Man's Land
programme.desc: A mystery man saves the life of a lieutenant and then disappears from the scene. Later on, the NCIS team unravels a new case when forensic evidence suggests he is a soldier who was captured in Afghanistan years ago and never found.

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans: Second Chances
programme.desc: After tracking stolen navy TNT to a cocaine warehouse, Special Agent Pride and the team uncover a domestic ring that is producing their own cocaine and is headed by Sonja's childhood friend.

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans: Radio Silence
programme.desc: The team protects a local DJ from a threatening perpetrator and investigates the murder of a navy captain who was attacked while on-air with the radio show. Also, Pride's daughter, Laurel, returns home to discuss her future plans.

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans: If It Bleeds, It Leads

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans: Means to An End
programme.desc: The NCIS team discovers a surveillance van filled with photos documenting Special Agent Pride's every move after his daughter is attacked on campus and placed in protective custody.

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans: Second Line
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a Navy lieutenant during a French Quarter second line funeral procession, a New Orleans tradition that celebrates the recently deceased with a musical parade.

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans: Collateral Damage
programme.desc: After a navy lieutenant dies during a clandestine visit to a general's hotel room, Special Agent Pride and the team are asked to break standard protocol for the murder investigation.

programme.title: Movie: Batman & Robin

programme.title: Movie: Batman
programme.desc: A masked vigilante, dressed like a bat, defends Gotham City from a madman known as The Joker.

programme.title: Movie: Batman Returns
programme.desc: Batman is called upon to rescue the citizens of Gotham City from the Penguin and Catwoman.

programme.desc: Threatened by an approaching wildfire, Stuart and Toni Rinker need a quick solution to their failing Idaho homestead. The Raneys plan to fire-proof the Rinkers' cabin and build a firebreak.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue: High Country Bear Lair
programme.desc: The Raneys build an old style root cellar and an innovative greenhouse and attempt to inspire two off-grid reluctant teenage sisters to help bring their father's dream to life.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue: Honey I Torched the Homestead
programme.desc: Fire is a constant threat for this Oregon homestead. Livestock should be meat, but here they are family pets. The Raneys must teach a hungry family to working together before it’s too late.

programme.title: Major League Fishing: 2019 World Championship: Sudden Death Round
programme.desc: Six anglers have survived the Elimination rounds and will face off in the Sudden Death round. The first four anglers to reach the target weight will advance to the Championship round.

programme.desc: A survival-savvy yoga teacher and a custom knife maker tackle the brutal jungle of Colombia. Their primitive survival skills are impressive, but the deadly spiders and tics test both their courage and resilience, threatening to take them down.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: Fan Down
programme.desc: A freak injury puts super-fan survivalists Maci and Justin at risk as they attempt to survive the treacherous Nicaraguan jungle for 14 days.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: Unhinged

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: Pick Your Poison

programme.title: The Simpsons: Loan-a-Lisa

programme.title: The Simpsons: MoneyBart

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Vegan Plant
programme.desc: Michael feeds a cannibalistic plant at a garden center, and at the tuxedo store, he sells a tie that literally suits itself. Plus, at a hockey rink, Michael's assistant is speechless with a trick so electric it melts the ice.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Sorcery or Science
programme.desc: Michael sweetens a woman's day at a candy store when a chocolate bunny eats her ring, and at the stadium for the Chicago White Sox, he inflates pretzels at a snack stand. Plus, one of Michael's tricks gets flushed down the tubes.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Laundry Day
programme.desc: The guys look for partners in crime at a supermarket and throw caution to the wind as helpless romantics. Plus, tonight’s loser has a serious wardrobe malfunction.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Mime and Punishment
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Murr and Q put the nasty in namaste when they lead guided meditations. Tonight's big loser gets boxed in with a 24-hour punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Junk in the Trunk
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Q and Murr feel the wrath of disapproving focus groups after nonsensical presentations, and try to reach out and touch strangers at the mall with anything but their hands. Plus, the guys must quit their job in front of a crowd.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Damned If You Do
programme.desc: Find out which joker once included inline skating as a skill on his resume, who was a mathlete and a bowler in high school and which joker was afraid of the dark as a child.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Up Loser's Creek
programme.desc: Find out which joker ran for Congress as a write-in candidate, which joker was a top-notch salesman before starting the show and who had a job in finance.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Rubbed the Wrong Way
programme.desc: Find out why the food court was picked for the 'stalk and text' challenge, what classic comedy inspired 'over the shoulder,' and who was lucky enough to test out the flying contraption for Joe the Genie.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Baggage Shame
programme.desc: Find out how Murr's old neighbour learns a little TMI about his personal life and which of the jokers Joe would want by his side in a fight.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Weight Loss
programme.desc: Adam illustrates all the reasons low-fat diets can actually make you fatter, and why counting calories is a waste of time.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Nutrition
programme.desc: Adam shows why vitamin supplements don't make us any healthier, and that the 'balanced breakfast' we've been sold is anything but. Later, he reveals how most of the nutritional science the media reports on isn't science at all.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Spider Man
programme.desc: Find out which of the jokers is the handiest, who hates the taste of beer, and how Q's love of animals beat out his arachnophobia and made for a better punishment.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine: The Funeral
programme.desc: The Nine-Nine gets another personnel change that could put Jake and Amy's relationship at risk. Meanwhile, Terry assists Holt with an important public relations matter. Rosa and Gina try to convince Charles to rethink his affection for a fellow officer.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine: Boyle's Hunch
programme.desc: Jake thinks Boyle might have met his soulmate in Genevieve, an art gallery owner, and he tries to help set them up. Meanwhile, Rosa deals with inter-precinct theft and Holt seeks Amy's help with an image campaign.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine: The Oolong Slayer
programme.desc: When investigating a serial killer, Jake enlists Holt's help in an attempt to solve the case off the radar. At the precinct, Rosa and Amy are forced to deal with demands from an old adversary, and Terry discovers a new obsession.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine: Halloween III
programme.desc: The third installment of Captain Holt and Jake's Halloween heist culminates with a tie-breaking competition to claim the title of 'amazing detective slash genius.' This year, Jake and Holt split the squad into two teams to help them win.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine: Into the Woods
programme.desc: Jake and Charles convince Terry to take a weekend camping trip as a needed 'babymoon,' but they soon realize it might not be the idealistic weekend they had hoped. Amy enlists Gina’s help to prepare her for an important presentation.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine: The Mattress
programme.desc: Jake and Amy convince the Captain to let them work a case together involving a new street drug, but their new relationship gets in the way. Meanwhile, at the precinct, Charles panics when he realizes he's damaged one of Holt's prized possessions.

programme.title: Tacoma FD: Full Moon Fever
programme.desc: FD braces for a night of insane full moon rescue calls. Plus, Eddie and Grannie go on active appendage duty and Ike gets bitten into an altered state.

programme.title: Tacoma FD: Old Flame
programme.desc: All New Eddie runs into his cradle-robbing first love and must decide whether to rekindle his romance with the sexy senior citizen. Then, Granny and Lucy compete to win the affections of an androgynous dispatcher and Terry discovers moisturizer.

programme.title: Laff Mobb's Laff Tracks: Soul Mom
programme.desc: Joyelle is done with dating actors, Daniel Weingarten accepts a challenge from his parents, and Yamaneika explains just how hard it is to run these streets. Plus, Rachel Feinstein's mom has soul in the 'joke of the night.'

programme.title: Laff Mobb's Laff Tracks: Worst Laid Plans
programme.desc: Rachel Feinstein, Tony Baker and Nate Jackson perform.

programme.title: Laff Mobb's Laff Tracks: The Inappropriate Barber
programme.desc: Mike Troy, Cee Jay Craxx and Paul Elia perform.

programme.title: Laff Mobb's Laff Tracks: Juicy
programme.desc: Ron G, Steven Briggs, Correy Bell and Neko White perform.

programme.title: Laff Mobb's Laff Tracks: The Laffs Are Back
programme.desc: Clayton English recalls his nightmare day job and Kira Soltanovich has a screaming match with her toddler son. Plus, Kerry Coddett gets catcalled by a toothless panhandler and Daniel Tirado daydreams about ditching his phone.

programme.title: Laff Mobb's Laff Tracks: Fingering the Man
programme.desc: Molly Austin takes a bachelorette party to the next level, and Jonathan Martin's parents give new meaning to the word 'cheap'. Plus, if you thought your family was embarrassing, Rachel Feinstein's might have them beat.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Smartest Inventions 3
programme.desc: Inventions include a blanket that absorbs flatulence, a wig made especially for cats, and a treadmill on wheels that finally makes it possible to take your indoor jogging routine outside.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Smartest Inventions 4
programme.desc: Inventions include a bra that doubles as a gas mask, a bib used for eating while driving and slippers with headlights.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: World's Smartest Inventions 5
programme.desc: Questionably brilliant innovations in portable toilets, beer-finding technology, weapon storage and wigs for newborns.

programme.title: World's Dumbest...: Smartest Inventions 6
programme.desc: Stars offer hilarious commentaries while viewing footage of the world's dumbest people.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: Japanese Waterless Folding Toilet
programme.desc: Find out which celebrity Michael named one of his wigs after and which trick required Michael's hidden hip-hop talents.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: An Elevated Ferret Alert
programme.desc: Find out which trick made it hard for Michael to walk the next day, what trick was added at the last minute after a prop broke and what animal Michael fosters during shoots.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: A Faux Pas to Wear
programme.desc: Why Michael says "shenanigans" during some tricks, his favorite trick of all time and why the crew had to shut down production for an hour.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Inside Carbonaro: The Framing Canon
programme.desc: Find out which magic team member knows how to ride a unicycle, what trick almost used an alpaca and which trick nearly resulted in Michael slicing open his neck.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Drugs
programme.desc: Adam weeds through the myths of marijuana, exposes the blunt truth about the War on Drugs, and explains how prescription pills are the true gateway drug.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Prison
programme.desc: Adam and Eve shed light on jail rehabilitation, solitary confinement and the fact that corporations make money off of prison inmates.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins the Wild West
programme.desc: Adam illustrates how, despite its macho man reputation, prostitutes actually helped settle the West. He then goes on to explain why the 'All-American Cowboy' is a myth, and that the real hero of the west wasn't a person at all - it was air conditioning.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Justice
programme.desc: After getting Emily out of prison, Adam and his sister Rhea take on the justice system, shining a spotlight on the infamous McDonalds hot coffee lawsuit, and the substantial problems with the jury duty system.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Having a Baby
programme.desc: Adam dispels the myth about getting pregnant after 35, delves into breastfeeding vs. formula, and explores postpartum depression for women, and men.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Art
programme.desc: Adam poses the question of what makes great art and shows why pieces visible to the masses are revered, regardless of artistic merit. He exposes the masters as copycats, and reveals that today's art market is a moneymaking scheme.

programme.desc: The guys pose as employees at a copy centre, conduct bizarre exit interviews for moviegoers and then lead some very peculiar protests. Later, it's unhappy hour for tonight's big loser, as he cuts off paying customers at a party.

programme.desc: Joe, Murr, Sal and Q get in a pickle serving up humiliating tasks as deli waiters, and later, they step behind the counter asking customers to settle a series of silly debates. Plus, tonight's big loser gets burned during an embarrassing toast.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Pseudo-Sumo
programme.desc: The Jokers that are big pro wrestling fans, and how long it took Joe to recover from his Sumo punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Uncool and the Gang
programme.desc: What was Sal's fear when Joe poured water in his mouth blindfolded? What punishmnet had producers so scared, they doubled security.

programme.title: Good Talk with Anthony Jeselnik: David Spade
programme.desc: David Spade explains what makes stand-up more difficult today, chats about steering clear of political jokes and considers how his childhood influenced his comedy.

programme.title: South Park: Krazy Kripples
programme.desc: Believing that people who are born disabled are better than those who are disabled through an accident, Jimmy and Timmy pull together a group of people like themselves and call their new club the 'Crips.'

programme.title: South Park: You Have 0 Friends
programme.desc: When Kyle begs Stan to 'friend' him, Stan gets sucked into the world of Facebook. Kyle gets a hard lesson about the politics of Facebook when he friends the least popular kid in school.

programme.title: South Park: Summer Sucks
programme.desc: The entire town is gearing up for the annual Fourth of July celebration when a state-wide ban on fireworks is imposed. Jimbo and Ned cross the border into Mexico to get some Grade-A illegal fireworks.

programme.title: South Park: Jakovasaurs
programme.desc: The boys discover a prehistoric creature called a Jackovasaur while camping at Stark's Pond.

programme.title: South Park: The Death of Eric Cartman
programme.desc: Cartman is sure he's dead and Butters is sure that Eric Cartman's ghost is haunting him. Together they attempt to make amends to all the people Cartman has wronged over the years.

programme.title: South Park: Chickenpox
programme.desc: The kids' parents only have their best interests at heart when they arrange for Stan, Kyle and Cartman to be exposed to the chickenpox virus. When the boys find out, they execute a plan to give their parents a virus as well: herpes.

programme.title: South Park: Smug Alert!
programme.desc: Kyle and his family are moving to San Francisco. The only way Stan can get his best friend back is to convince everyone to start driving hybrid cars.

programme.title: South Park: Britney's New Look
programme.desc: When the boys help Britney Spears get to the North Pole, they discover the shocking secret behind her popularity.

programme.title: South Park: Conjoined Fetus Lady
programme.desc: Kyle visits the dreaded school nurse, who suffers from Conjoined Twin Myslexia Disease. His reaction prompts the entire town to rally behind the nurse, singling her out to 'honour' her. Meanwhile, Pip leads the school dodgeball team to the championships.

programme.title: South Park: Toilet Paper
programme.desc: Cartman convinces the boys to toilet paper their teacher's house. But when Kyle is overwhelmed with guilt for the horrendous prank, Cartman tries to kill him.

programme.title: King of the Hill: Phish and Wild Life
programme.desc: When Hank takes Bobby and the guys fishing, they camp out next to a bunch of hippies.

programme.title: King of the Hill: Cheer Factor
programme.desc: When Peggy becomes an assistant cheerleading coach she butts heads with the head coach.

programme.title: South Park: Terrance and Phillip in 'Not Without My Anus'
programme.desc: Terrance and Phillip must travel to Iran to save Terrance's little daughter, Sally. While they are gone Saddam Hussein takes over Canada, leaving Terrance and Phillip to save the entire country.

programme.title: South Park: Chickenlover
programme.desc: During a crime spree, Barbrady is forced to admit he can't read and must return to school. Anarchy ensues when the town realizes they are without law enforcement, so Barbrady makes the boys deputies to help keep the peace.

programme.title: South Park: Ike's Wee Wee
programme.desc: Mr. Mackey, the school counsellor, is fired and turns to drugs and alcohol. Meanwhile, when the boys find out what it means to be circumcised they try to save Ike from his Bris.

programme.title: South Park: Chef's Chocolate Salty Balls
programme.desc: South Park's first film festival attracts crowds of pretentious, tofu-eating movie lovers to the quiet mountain town. The influx of raw vegetable waste taxes the sewage system putting Mr. Hankey's life in jeopardy.

programme.title: South Park: Chef Aid
programme.desc: A record company steals Chef's song 'Stinky Britches' and he sues them for the rights. When Johnny Cochrane defends the record company, Chef is left penniless. But he has some very famous and talented friends who want to help their old mentor.

programme.title: South Park: Gnomes
programme.desc: Cartman, Stan, Kyle and Kenny are assigned to write a report with Tweek, the very nervous and highly caffeinated boy who insists gnomes are stealing his underpants.

programme.title: South Park: The Succubus
programme.desc: Chef's parents arrive in South Park from Scotland fresh from an encounter with the Loch Ness Monster to attend Chef's wedding. However, the boys believe Chef's bride-to-be is a succubus, and they try to stop the wedding.

programme.title: South Park: Tweek vs. Craig
programme.desc: The boys instigate a fight between Tweek and Craig in shop class. Meanwhile, Mr. Adler, the shop teacher, is haunted by a recurring dream of his lost love.

programme.title: South Park: Sexual Harassment Panda
programme.desc: After Sexual Harassment Panda 'educates' the children, Cartman sues Stan for sexual harassment, and a flurry of other lawsuits follow.

programme.title: South Park: Cat Orgy
programme.desc: While all the adults are gathering at Mr. Mackey's house to watch a meteor shower Shelly Marsh is left baby-sit Cartman. They square off when she invites her much older boyfriend over.

programme.title: South Park: Two Guys Naked in a Hot Tub
programme.desc: Stan's parents drag him along to Mr. Mackey's meteor shower party, where he is sent down into the basement to play with a 'bunch of Melvins.' The ATF believes everyone in the house to be part of a cult, and Stan and the 'Angels' must save the day.

programme.title: South Park: Jewbilee
programme.desc: Kyle invites Kenny to join him and Ike at Jewbilee, a camp for Jewish kids, where they make various arts and crafts as tribute to the great and powerful Moses.

programme.title: South Park: Chinpoko Mon
programme.desc: Stan, Kyle, Cartman and Kenny are caught up in the latest fad from Japan: Chinpoko Mon. Little do they know, they are being brainwashed by the Japanese to once again bomb Pearl Harbor.

programme.title: South Park: Cartman's Silly Hate Crime 2000
programme.desc: Cartman is pursued by the FBI for committing a hate crime against Token and lands in juvenile hall.

programme.title: South Park: Timmy! 2000
programme.desc: When Timmy is diagnosed with Attention Deficit Disorder, it triggers a wave of prescription drug abuse at South Park Elementary. With all the spare time he has now, Timmy joins a rock band as the lead singer.

programme.title: South Park: Cartman Joins NAMBLA
programme.desc: Cartman suddenly finds Stan, Kenny and Kyle too immature to be his friends. He then decides to seek friendship using the Internet, where he finds an older man who is more than willing to be his 'friend.'

programme.title: South Park: Chef Goes Nanners
programme.desc: Chef's passionate protest declaring the South Park flag is racist enflames the entire town, including the kids.

programme.title: Movie: Friday
programme.desc: Two friends must come up with $200, to pay drug dealer Big Worm for marijuana they smoked.

programme.title: Movie: Next Friday
programme.desc: A streetwise man visits his cousin and his uncle, who have recently won the lottery.

programme.title: Movie: Bad Boys
programme.desc: Two hip Miami undercover detectives have 72 hours to nab the crooks who stole millions in recovered heroin from the police station.

programme.title: Movie: American Gangster
programme.desc: A detective in 1970s America works to bring down the drug empire of Frank Lucas. The kingpin from Manhattan is smuggling the drug into the country from the Far East.

programme.title: Movie: All Eyez on Me
programme.desc: The true story of musician Tupac Shakur's life. Raised by activists, the young black man grew up to be a poet, actor and one of the world's most successful rappers whose impact has only grown since his death, turning him into a global cultural icon.

programme.desc: Hawkeye is confronted with death when he is unable to save an old journalist friend.

programme.title: M*A*S*H: Dear Dad ... Again
programme.desc: Hawkeye writes to his father about Frank, Hot Lips, and a doctor who is an impostor.

programme.desc: Opie gets a new bicycle when he comes home with an all-A report card.

programme.title: The Andy Griffith Show: Up in Barney's Room
programme.desc: Barney gets evicted from his rooming house and takes over the back office at work.

programme.title: The Andy Griffith Show: A Date for Gomer
programme.desc: Barney sets Gomer up with his girlfriend's cousin.

programme.desc: Barney tickets Gomer for an illegal turn, then Gomer arrests Barney for the same thing.

programme.desc: Ray and Debra get marriage counseling after arguing about Ray's inability to do housework.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond: Homework
programme.desc: Ray attends an open school night after complaining that Ally has too much homework.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond: Pet the Bunny
programme.desc: Although his father is alive and well, Raymond decides to write his eulogy.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond: Who Am I?
programme.desc: After Ray and Debra spend an evening apart, Ray admits he no longer enjoys his nights out.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Help Daddy Find His Toenail
programme.desc: Charlie's girlfriend is embarrassed to be seen with him in public. Meanwhile, Jake sneaks out to a concert despite Alan's forbiddance.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: The Leather Gear Is in the Guest Room
programme.desc: Charlie and Alan butt heads over Alan's place in the house, prompting Alan and Jake to move in with Evelyn.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Patrons Ain't
programme.desc: Doug and Carrie donate $500 to the school library and get their names put on a plaque.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Eddie Money
programme.desc: After Doug and Deacon win $5,000 gambling, Doug tries to hide the money from Carrie.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Two Thirty
programme.desc: When Doug needs dental work, he discovers that Carrie used to have a crush on his dentist.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Shrink Wrap
programme.desc: Carrie and Doug decide to send Arthur to a psychotherapist to find the root of his anger.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Is There a Mrs. Waffles?
programme.desc: Children's music will never be the same after Charlie becomes an unlikely children's music sensation. When he refuses to perform at a concert, his record promoter threatens to sue him.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: Tight's Good (Shoes, Hats, Pickle Jar Lids)
programme.desc: Charlie tries to resist his strong attraction to his soon-to-be stepsister.

programme.title: The Goldbergs: Mama Drama
programme.desc: Adam's desire to be the lead in the school musical 'Jesus Christ Superstar' goes unfulfilled when he's cast in something other than the leading role. Insistent that her son is a star, Beverly casts Adam in a rival show, but he becomes too self-obsessed.

programme.title: The Goldbergs: The Facts of Bleeping Life
programme.desc: With Prince Charles and Lady Di's wedding approaching, Beverly gets the idea for her and Murray to renew their vows. But with Murray's complete lack of interest, will Beverly lose her chance at a second wedding?

programme.title: Roseanne: Of Ice and Men
programme.desc: Dan takes to the ice to help D.J. win a position on the Junior Hockey League team, but D.J.'s position is generally on the floor. Meanwhile, Roseanne's patience is put to the test helping Jackie recover from a broken romance.

programme.title: Roseanne: Crime and Punishment
programme.desc: Dan is summoned to D.J.'s principal's office when he is caught with obscene reading materials - which he got from Darlene! Meanwhile, Roseanne learns a shocking secret when she presses Jackie about spending time with Fisher.

programme.title: Roseanne: War and Peace
programme.desc: Dan's in jail for beating up Jackie's boyfriend, Fisher, and Darlene relishes the moment when she has to bail him out. Meanwhile, Roseanne convinces Jackie to leave her abusive boyfriend.

programme.title: Roseanne: Lanford Daze
programme.desc: D.J.preps for a school play, Dan tries his hand at selling hot tubs, and Darlene demonstrates her animal rights stance by lashing out against Roseanne's business.

programme.title: Roseanne: Wait 'Til Your Father Gets Home
programme.desc: After their father dies, Roseanne and Jackie cope with their loss in very different ways.

programme.title: Roseanne: First Cousin, Twice Removed
programme.desc: Roseanne's rich and snooty cousin, Ronnie, makes an unannounced visit to Lanford. She reignites a long-simmering feud with Roseanne when she advises Darlene to leave home to pursue her dreams.

programme.title: Roseanne: Lose a Job, Winnebago
programme.desc: In her neighbors' motor home, Roseanne spearheads a trip to Hollywood where they all attend a taping of The Jackie Thomas Show. Meanwhile, Dan is determined to convince Roseanne to have another child.

programme.title: Roseanne: It's a Boy!
programme.desc: Darlene wants her boyfriend, David, to move in with the Conners. Roseanne is against the idea until she sees what David's home life is like. Now she has to convince Dan to let him stay.

programme.title: Roseanne: It Was Twenty Years Ago Today
programme.desc: Roseanne plans to set off some sparks by surprising Dan with a sexy boudoir photograph to mark their 20th wedding anniversary. But Dan's surprise gift to Roseanne results in major marital fireworks.

programme.title: Roseanne: Playing With Matches
programme.desc: Roseanne's mom makes a new friend, Jake, at the retirement home. Now, Roseanne's playing Cupid and Jackie's planning their wedding. With special guest Red Buttons.

programme.title: Roseanne: Promises, Promises
programme.desc: Darlene and David's youthful romance takes a complicated turn when she surprises him by renting a hotel room after their senior prom. Meanwhile, Roseanne and Dan decide to invest in a friend's business scheme.

programme.title: Roseanne: Glengarry, Glen Rosey
programme.desc: The old house Roseanne and Dan fixed up to sell languishes on the market after Roger abruptly skips town.

programme.title: Roseanne: Tooth or Consequences
programme.desc: Darlene must choose between David and her future when she gets accepted to a college for the arts in Chicago. Roseanne gets a visit from the new health inspector: her old boss Leon.

programme.title: Roseanne: Daughters and Other Strangers
programme.desc: Dan and Roseanne decide to let Darlene go to art school in Chicago, but will Darlene leave David to pursue her future? Meanwhile, at a birthday party, 'Seven Minutes in Heaven' turns DJ into a new man.

programme.title: Roseanne: Two Down, One to Go

programme.desc: The deathbed confession of an ex-patient at Shady Pines means bad news for Sophia, since in her confession she stated that she and Sophia started the 1985 Shady Pines fire.

programme.title: Frasier: Liar, Liar

programme.title: Frasier: Three Days of the Condo
programme.desc: When his request to install a doorknocker is denied, Frasier challenges the condo association president, Ms. Langer, by running against her in the election.

programme.title: Frasier: Death and the Dog
programme.desc: When Eddie becomes lethargic and refuses to eat, Martin tries everything to perk him up. He hires a dog psychiatrist to determine the cause of this depression.

programme.title: Movie: The Last Bridesmaid
programme.desc: A woman is focused on taking over her family business, instead of looking for a man. All her friends are getting married and she seems to get new invitations in the mail weekly. While at a wedding, she meets a documentarian, and he changes her life.

programme.title: Movie: The Perfect Bride: Wedding Bells
programme.desc: Newly engaged Nick and Molly are looking forward to their big day. After their overbooked wedding venue reserves the wrong date, they have six weeks to plan the wedding of their dreams.

programme.desc: A blogger with a string of disappointing Valentine's Days is ready to give up on love when she meets Brenden, a handsome veterinarian. She finds out he blames his recent break-up on her blog and he is the one leaving her angry comments.

programme.desc: Beautiful six-year-old okapi, K'Tusha, is nearing the end of her first pregnancy. Keepers are hoping her first baby will help to conserve this highly-endangered species from the rainforests of central Africa.

programme.title: Tanked!: The Hoff Gets Tanked
programme.desc: As an avid diver, David Hasselhoff would love a shark tank in his home, but after an inspirational visit to the Aquarium of the Pacific, ATM get some bad news.

programme.title: Tanked!: Shark Buffet
programme.desc: Wayde and Brett travel to Alabama to build a giant centre bar aquarium for the owners of Creek Casino.

programme.title: Tanked!: Gangster Tank
programme.desc: The Mob Museum makes Wayde and Brett an offer they can't refuse. They want a tank with replica sunken rumrunner for their prohibition exhibit and speakeasy in downtown Las Vegas.

programme.title: Tanked!: Hang Ten Barbeque
programme.desc: The guys bring the ocean to life with a pipeline wave tank, a new shape for ATM. Home in Vegas, the guys create a smoker tank for The Rolling Smoke BBQ.

programme.title: Tanked!: Brett's Donut Mania Tank
programme.desc: Donut Mania is Brett's newest business venture and they are about to have their grand opening. Wayde and the whole team want to congratulate him with a surprise fish tank.

programme.title: Tanked!: Howie Mandel's Surprise Tank
programme.desc: Howie Mandel has reached out to ATM for another tank. Only this time it's a surprise for his manager's company. 3 Arts Entertainment already has an existing tank but it needs a serious upgrade.

programme.title: Tanked!: The Amazing Piano Tank
programme.desc: Pianoworks in Rochester, NY wants Wayde and Brett to build them a piano of their own. This one is made out of acrylic and filled with fish of course.

programme.title: Tanked!: Give a Dog a Phone
programme.desc: A business communications company is in need of an interactive aquarium shaped like a giant telephone. Later, the ATM crew have to build a tank that can be used as a dog house.

programme.title: The Zoo: The Marvelous Mott Mott
programme.desc: Max, a beloved camel, develops a large abscess on the top of his head. An adorable marmot prepares for her first classroom visit as an ambassador animal. Later, a peregrine falcon found at the base of skyscraper is bought to the zoo for a health check.

programme.title: The Zoo: A Sea Lion Pup Grows Up
programme.desc: Zoo staff face unexpected setbacks weaning a bright sea lion pup off her mother's milk. The bird team steps in to support a rejected curassow chick. And, a squirrel monkey pays a visit to the dentist.

programme.desc: New discoveries reveal the scandals and secrets of Nero and his lost palace. Using cutting-edge technology, experts may confirm if the infamous emperor burned down Rome so his could build his golden wonder.

programme.title: Unearthed: Mysteries in the Alien Desert
Unearthed: Finding Sparta's 300
programme.desc: New discoveries might lead investigators to the legendary group of 300 Spartan warriors who died fighting off one million invading Persians, and using cutting-edge tech, experts reconstruct the lost battlefield to uncover the secrets of their last stand.

programme.title: Unearthed: Forbidden City of the Pharaohs
programme.desc: A mysterious city in ancient Egypt is the home of the Valley of the Kings and grand temples, and new discoveries reveal how they were built and if the god the pharaohs worshiped here helped them rise to power.

programme.title: Unearthed: Mystery of Egypt's Mega Temple
programme.desc: The Egyptian mega-temple at Karnak was once the political powerbase of the ancient pharaohs and the home to strange religious rituals. New discoveries of skeletons tell a story of jealousy, betrayal, and the rise of a new religion that changed Egypt.

programme.title: Unearthed: Curse of the Seventh Wonder
Unearthed: Pompeii's Evil Twin
programme.desc: The lost city of Baiae was the Las Vegas of the ancient world, until a mysterious calamity caused it to sink into the sea. New discoveries reveal the secrets of this sin city that, like Pompeii, some experts believe was destroyed by a supervolcano.

programme.title: Unearthed: Mystery of the Ice Mummy
programme.desc: The Iceman is the world's oldest and most perfectly preserved mummy, and brand-new cutting-edge forensic technology is revealing his darkest secrets; these discoveries could change everything we know about the humans who lived 5,000 years ago.

programme.title: Unearthed: Dead Sea Scrolls: The Dark Truth
programme.desc: The Dead Sea Scrolls are one of the Holy Land's greatest mysteries, but new discoveries might finally reveal the hidden meaning behind the strange texts, and as experts get closer to unmasking their author, they find clues of an ancient apocalypse.

programme.title: America Unearthed: The Ripper Unmasked
programme.desc: Scott Wolter investigates a sensational new theory about the identity of Jack the Ripper. He also uncovers a connection between the ritualistic patterns of the Ripper murders and the Freemasons.

programme.title: America Unearthed: Cave of Secrets
programme.desc: Scott Wolter travels to Shippensburg, Pennsylvania, to check out a mysterious cave that may have been a stop on the Underground Railroad.

programme.title: America Unearthed: The Spy Who Saved America
programme.desc: Scott Wolter leaves no stone unturned in his quest to discover the true identity of Agent 355, a mysterious female operative who was a crucial member of General George Washington's Culper Spy Ring.

programme.title: America Unearthed: Chicago's Mystery Bomber
programme.desc: Forensic geologist Scott Wolter turns detective to crack one of Chicago's oldest cold cases -- the 1886 bombing of Haymarket Square. A recent dig for a buried time capsule may yield new clues about the identity of the bomber.

programme.title: The Squad: Prison Police: Extortion
programme.desc: The Squad believes that prison gang members are running an extortion game when an inmate's family receives violent threats.

programme.title: Why I Ran: Scott/ Trey
programme.desc: A man starts new life only to find himself caught up in a high-speed pursuit due to drugs.

programme.title: Why I Ran: Justin/ Frank
programme.desc: A struggling father resorts to illegal means to make a living, leading him into a situation involving stolen property; a doting grandfather ends up on the wrong side of the law and flees after being pulled over

programme.title: Why I Ran: Maston/ Young
programme.desc: A man who had it all loses everything after his wife leaves him, taking their only child with her. Despondent over the loss, he robs a gas station and then leads police on a high-speed chase through the city streets of Indianapolis.

programme.title: The Menendez Murders: Erik Tells All: A Murder in Beverly Hills
programme.desc: Erik Menendez himself shares in stunning detail the events of the week leading up to the murders of his parents and the motive behind the crime that shocked America.

programme.title: The Menendez Murders: Erik Tells All: A Family With a Dirty Secret
programme.desc: The trial of the century takes a shocking turn when the defense says Erik and Lyle Menendez killed their parents after years of sexual abuse. Erik tells his harrowing account of growing up in the Menendez 'house of horrors.'

programme.title: The Menendez Murders: Erik Tells All: Abuse Excuse?
programme.desc: Erik Menendez recalls his mother's decline in the years leading up to the murders and looks on in agony as his brother, Lyle testifies, vividly describing the abuse at the hands of their father.

programme.desc: The first trial ends in a stunning surprise verdict. Erik takes us inside the prep for his second trial, his unlikely connection with OJ Simpson, and why he alone is left to speak for both the brothers as another jury debates life in prison or death.

programme.title: The Menendez Murders: Erik Tells All: Final Verdict
programme.desc: After the verdict comes in, at the last minute, Erik Menendez and his brother, Lyle, are sent to separate prisons. Erik marries and begins to mount an appeal.

programme.title: The Big House: Parchman
programme.desc: Originally a slave plantation ruled by the whip, today Parchman's most important crop is neither food nor cotton, but prisoners.

programme.title: The Big House: Montana State Prison

programme.title: The Big House: Maine State Prison
programme.desc: Many changes have occurred in Maine State Prison since its opening in 1824.

programme.title: Rookies: Tampa: It's a 'Deadly' Profession
programme.desc: April gets two 'Signal 7' calls and has to bag human bones as well as lift a five day old dead body complete with maggots and flies. Tommie Talbot is confronted about his poor driving and blames it on his pigeon-toed feet.

programme.title: Rookies: Jefferson: One More Time
programme.desc: Mark Monson gets assessed by his sergeant for the second time. If he passes this time, he may begin to ride solo and get his own car. If he fails again, his dream of becoming a cop will be in jeopardy

programme.title: Rookies: Tampa: Command Presence
programme.desc: Amy has issues with German Shepherds and being attacked by a K-9 is part of training.

programme.title: Rookies: Jefferson: Rookie Love
programme.desc: Rebecca is ten weeks in to her field training and makes a potentially fatal mistake. She and her boyfriend - also a rookie - must learn to deal with the realities that come with the dangerous job.

programme.title: Rookies: Tampa: Size Doesn't Matter
programme.desc: Rookie Amy Hess proves size doesn't matter after finishing a week where she pushes a car and participates in her first SWAT hit. Tommie Tolbert tries to keep an arson victim from attacking the assailant's mother.

programme.title: Rookies: Jefferson: Riding Solo
programme.desc: Henry Conravey has successfully completed 12 weeks of field training but after a series of mistakes is not sure he'll be allowed to patrol the streets on his own. Chris Cade is looking forward to getting is own patrol car.

programme.title: Model Killers: Finger-Licking Good/ Slaying of a Tennessee Beauty Queen
programme.desc: A woman meets the perfect man to launch her modeling career, but is soon faced with a brutal reality. A beautiful farm girl moves to the city to pursue her modeling dreams, but winds up living a nightmare.

programme.title: Model Killers: Reality Kills
programme.desc: Three passionate, attractive men - and some very adoring fans - lust after a swimsuit model with a heart of gold.

programme.title: Model Killers: Model Murderer/ Strangled Beauty
programme.desc: An ambitious male model with a physique to die for is about to become an international sensation -- but not in a way he could have foreseen.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: The Monster I Married
programme.desc: When Cindy first fell in love with Bobby, she never imagined that she would one day regret not committing murder. But years after she first discovered the horrifying truth, she is still haunted by the night when she decided not to kill him in his sleep.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: Master Manipulator
programme.desc: When Brian Cargill divorced his wife, Kimberly, he thought it was the end of the worst time of his life. Instead, he found himself in a desperate battle to save his son, Jamie, from her deadly wrath.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Murder by Design
programme.desc: Investigators are looking into the killing spree that ended the life of Gianni Versace in Miami 20 years ago.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Blood in the Sand
programme.desc: The murder of two teen girls on the same California beach, six years apart, shocked residents of San Diego when it happened. The DNA uncovered in the case, decades later, would stun them a second time when it led to someone inside the police department.

programme.title: Murder Board: Murdered by Numbers
programme.desc: Detectives are presented an unusual problem, dozens of people witness a deadly attack, but no one can identify the gang members involved.

programme.title: Death in the Desert
programme.desc: A mysterious email drives a wedge between a mother and daughter. The situation goes from tense to tragic when the daughter is thrust into an investigation that will ultimately expose a cold-blooded murder and a secret life.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Target Justice
programme.desc: Investigating the attacks on a Texas prosecutor and district attorney who were both shot dead.

programme.title: Unusual Suspects: A Taste for Blood
programme.desc: A young mother's body is found in the bath in what police think is an accident. However, case quickly turns into a murder investigation. Two more victims are connected to the murderer, who cops must hunt down before he strikes again.

programme.title: Unusual Suspects: Down to the Wire
programme.desc: A mother of three grown children is stabbed in her own home in the middle of the day. Digging back through her life for motive, detectives are led to some unlikely suspects. It takes years, and one bold wiretap, to uncover what really happened to her.

programme.title: True Conviction: Drive to Kill
programme.desc: Cindy Albrecht, the head chef for one of the racing world's top teams, goes missing from her home near the INDY 500 and as the suspects start piling up it becomes a race against time.

programme.title: True Conviction: Protecting a Friend
programme.desc: Young mother Tyshika Askins is found dead in her home but after charging the wrong man, police knew the key to solving the case lies is an OJ container left out on a kitchen counter.

programme.title: True Conviction: Murder in New England
programme.desc: After grandmother Irene Kennedy is found dead in a park, her family is split apart when a distant relative is charged with her murder. It will take years, another killing and a jail house snitch to bring the family back together.

programme.title: Ice Cold Killers: Hitchike to Hell
programme.desc: Colorado police in hot pursuit of a serial killer are horrified when the pre-recorded message of a pager found in the snow near a victim's abandoned vehicle reveals an unthinkable predator: one sworn to protect and serve.

programme.title: Ice Cold Killers: Blizzard of Bullets
programme.desc: During chilling winter nights in Idaho, a madman is on the prowl... attacking citizens in a crime spree that stuns families and terrifies whole communities. Investigators must race against time to catch a killer... who kills for the thrill of it.

programme.title: Ice Cold Killers: Frozen Innocence
programme.desc: On a chilly winter morning in St. Louis, an eleven year old girl vanishes. Days later her body is found along an icy river bank. She's been brutally murdered. It's a race against time for investigators who fear the elusive killer will strike again.

programme.title: Fear Thy Neighbor: House of the Rising Gun
programme.desc: A single mother of four children finds the perfect house in New Orleans -- complete with a nice family next door. All is well until a robbery at the neighbor's home leads first to misunderstanding, then to paranoia, conflict and horrible bloodshed.

programme.title: Fear Thy Neighbor: Monkey in the Middle
programme.desc: A man finds himself living between hostile neighbors on both sides, including a former marine with severe PTSD. Their campaign of intimidation ramps up to the point of no return, ending with a deadly confrontation on the street outside their homes.

programme.title: Fear Thy Neighbor: Night of the Machete
programme.desc: Two young couples in an Idaho apartment building walk on eggshells around a neighbor who can't stand the one couple's music and the other's dog. Tensions keep rising between all parties until finally hell breaks loose and a violent rampage erupts.

programme.title: Fear Thy Neighbor: Neighbors on a Dead End
programme.desc: A disabled fireman appoints himself sheriff of his street, policing his neighbors and clamping down on violations ranging from loud music to off leash dogs. Things boil over at a birthday party where shots are fired and blood runs in the street.

programme.title: See No Evil: Dead Man Walking
programme.desc: In September 2015, Edward Lowry is found savagely beaten and stabbed to death in a parking lot in South Dakota. With little evidence left behind at the scene, can police painstakingly piece together Ed's last moments from CCTV to find his killer?

programme.title: I (Almost) Got Away With It: Got Caught Via Email
programme.desc: When Edward Roberson and two fellow inmates went to the showers, they didn't come back. Breaking out, the three scaled the fences and made a run for it. Roberson has escaped and now has stolen guns, in a stolen car running for his life.

programme.title: I (Almost) Got Away With It: Got to Roll the Dice
programme.desc: With 41 arrests and no convictions, Philip Williams had no worries that stabbing his ex in a parking lot would give him any trouble. But when he became a wanted man, he hit the road. Surviving on his gambling wins, Williams kept on the move.

programme.title: I (Almost) Got Away With It: Got to Be a Car Salesman
programme.desc: An ex-con rebuilds his reputation as a successful car salesman until a freak accident threatens to send him back to jail.

programme.title: I (Almost) Got Away With It: Got to Escape From the Rock
programme.desc: In 1987, an ex-con prepares for his most daring crime yet; ambushing a prison transport van.

programme.desc: An elderly woman puts up a fight when an ex-employee tries to steal her rings, and is murdered.

programme.desc: While facing five life sentences for a spree of Florida robberies, Frankie Lee Bass makes a daring escape and goes on the run.

programme.title: I (Almost) Got Away With It: Got to Run to Mexico With My Kids
programme.desc: In 2009, Valerie Castellano robbed a bank in rural Indiana and fled with her children and girlfriend to Mexico.

programme.desc: An elderly woman puts up a fight when an ex-employee tries to steal her rings, and is murdered.
